---
title: ""
format:
  html:
    toc: false
editor: source
---

<style>
.rs-container{
  max-width:1000px;
  margin:0 auto;
}

.lab-card{
  background:#ffffff;
  border-radius:18px;
  padding:1.4rem;
  margin-bottom:1.6rem;
  box-shadow:0 6px 18px rgba(0,0,0,0.07);
  border:1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.05);
}

.tag-row{
  display:flex;
  flex-wrap:wrap;
  gap:.45rem;
  margin-bottom:.7rem;
}

.tag{
  background:#eef4ff;
  padding:.25rem .7rem;
  border-radius:999px;
  font-size:.8rem;
  font-weight:600;
  color:#2457a6;
}

.project-link{
  font-weight:600;
  text-decoration:none;
}

.project-link:hover{
  text-decoration:underline;
}

.back-btn{
  display:inline-block;
  margin-bottom:1.4rem;
  padding:.55rem 1rem;
  border-radius:999px;
  background:#f3f4f6;
  color:#222;
  text-decoration:none;
  font-weight:600;
  border:1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.08);
}

.back-btn:hover{
  background:#e9ecef;
}
</style>

<div class="rs-container">

[← Back to Projects](content.html){.back-btn}


# Remote Sensing Projects

*Click a project below to view the full lab report.*

---

### Land Cover Classification

::: {.lab-card}

::: {.tag-row}
[Remote Sensing]{.tag}
[Image Classification]{.tag}
[Landsat]{.tag}
:::

This project applies supervised image classification to Landsat imagery using the Maximum Likelihood algorithm to map seven land-cover classes: Broadleaf Forest, Coniferous Forest, Exposed Soil and Rocks, High Density Developed, Low Density Developed, Non-forest Vegetation, and Water. The workflow includes training and validation polygons, spectral signature analysis, and accuracy assessment.

[See full lab report (PDF) →](labs/classification.pdf){.project-link}

:::

---

### LiDAR Forest Structure and Individual Tree Segmentation

::: {.lab-card}

::: {.tag-row}
[LiDAR]{.tag}
[Forest Structure]{.tag}
[Point Cloud]{.tag}
:::

This lab uses airborne LiDAR point cloud data to identify and segment individual trees in forest plots. The workflow includes plot extraction, canopy height model generation, tree-top detection, and segmentation using the **li2012** and **dalponte2016** algorithms, with comparison across multiple canopy height model resolutions to determine an efficient balance between detail and computation.

[See full lab report (PDF) →](labs/lidar.pdf){.project-link}

:::

---

### Spectral Indices and Vegetation Properties

::: {.lab-card}

::: {.tag-row}
[Spectral Analysis]{.tag}
[Vegetation Indices]{.tag}
[Remote Sensing]{.tag}
:::

This project analyzes reflectance spectra measured with the ASD FieldSpecPro to compare live vegetation, dead vegetation, soil, and rock. It also computes spectral indices such as **PRI, SIPI, and ND705**, simulates mixed pixels, and shows how **NDVI** and **NDWI** respond to increasing live vegetation cover at Landsat spectral resolution.

[See full lab report (PDF) →](labs/spectral.pdf){.project-link}

:::

---

### Wildfire Burn Severity and Vegetation Recovery

::: {.lab-card}

::: {.tag-row}
[Landsat]{.tag}
[Time Series]{.tag}
[NDVI / NBR]{.tag}
:::

This project analyzes wildfire burn severity and post-fire vegetation recovery using a Landsat 8 time series over the Prouton Lakes fire in British Columbia. The workflow includes cloud masking, NDVI and NBR calculation, yearly compositing, dNBR-based burn severity classification, and assessment of vegetation recovery patterns from 2018 to 2021.

[See full lab report (PDF) →](labs/wildfire.pdf){.project-link}

:::

</div>
